basics.name:
Skye Cassiopeia-Jacqueline Owen-Lloyd-Walters
MPhys MInstP FRAS
basics.label: Software Engineer

work.name: Canonical
work.summary: Primary owner of internal automation infrastructure, managing Jenkins CI pipelines, image promotion workflows, and automated point release and bug backporting processes.
highlights: Developed Terraform modules to bootstrap a complete MAAS topology from nothing on LXD, enabling infrastructure-as-code deployment workflows for field engineers.
Contributed to the MAAS Terraform Provider enabling CRUD management of MAAS resources (networking, users, machines, VM hosts) through HashiCorp's Terraform.
Maintained the MAAS Charms repository, developing charmed operators that automate operational procedures of running MAAS.
Worked on MAAS Anvil, a snap-based solution for managing charmed MAAS deployments with Terraform-driven configuration.
Created and delivered Terraform-driven Charmed MAAS training for the Berlin Commercial Sprint 2025, inducting field engineers in the new MAAS Anvil deployment approach.
Contributed to documentation linting tooling for the wider MAAS team, providing CLI wrappers around aspell, proselint, linkchecker, and style for automated doc quality checks.

work.name: Canonical
work.summary: Joined the MAAS team through Canonical's graduate fast track programme as a Python backend developer.
highlights: Developed and maintained MAAS Ansible Playbooks, providing Ansible-based bootstrapping and configuration for MAAS deployments.
Created the MAAS Ansible Collection, published to Ansible Galaxy, enabling resource management of MAAS environments through Ansible automation.
Contributed to the MAAS Terraform Provider, implementing OpenAPI-driven resource management for MAAS infrastructure.
Worked on the MAAS OpenAPI Schema, documenting the full MAAS REST API in OpenAPI Specification format for language-agnostic client generation.
Developed Packer templates for creating custom deployable OS images within MAAS environments.

courses: First year mean grade of 76.7%, Final year mean grade of 79.2%

Masters thesis combining computational modelling, first hand observation, and supplemental data to measure exoplanet transits. Developed analytical models of transit timing variation to investigate the orbital properties of non-transiting planets. Results were summarised with a 6000 word dissertation, 10 minute presentation/discussion, and scientific poster.

Bachelors thesis utilising computational modelling and signal processing to identify and model glitch events within the LIGO data set. Results were summarised with a 5000 word dissertation, 10 minute presentation/discussion, and LaTeX Compatible result list.

education.score: Certified in the practical application of AI and Data Engineering
courses: Completed the course to the highest standard awarded in less than half the expected time.
Software engineering: Git & GitHub, advanced Python, algorithms & data structures.
Data engineering: SQL, data lakes, data warehousing, web scraping with Selenium.
Data science: Data cleaning, preprocessing & visualisation, A/B testing, feature engineering, statistical modelling, model selection and implementation using sklearn.
Cloud engineering: cloud computing, designing and building APIs, Docker, Apache Airflow, AWS Serverless Stack (EC2, S3, RDS).

education.score: Physics (A), EPQ (A), Maths (B), Further Maths (D), AS-Chemistry (A)
courses: Produced a Level 3 Extended Project Qualification to a high standard titled `Is There A Possibility of Extrasolar Habitation?', studying the habitability of Earth-like exoplanets.

education.score: 9 GCSE's A* to B Including Science, Computer Science, Maths, and English

awards.title: Graham Bryant Prize for Best Observatory Project
awards.summary: First recipient of the award to honour the late Graham Bryant for my Masters thesis

awards.title: Certified in the practical application of AI and Data Engineering
awards.summary: Awarded for the successful completion of the course. Completed to the highest standard awarded in less than half the expected time.

memberships.name: Member
memberships.summary: Granted the use of the post-nomen `MInstP'

interests.name: Space & Spaceflight
keywords: Natural or artificial, if it's found outside of Earth's atmosphere, I probably like it. Exoplanets and deep space probes are my favourite. I keep an eye on every mission that leaves Earth.

interests.name: Reading
keywords: When I'm not staring at a terminal, I'm staring at a page. Current favourites are Project Hail Mary and The Hydrogen Sonata; I have a soft spot for first-contact and post-scarcity sci-fi that explores how we might grow as a species.

interests.name: Gaming
keywords: I Love Factorio, Stellaris, Transport Fever, modded Minecraft, and Kerbal Space Program — the latter three feature heavily in my YouTube content creation. There's something satisfying about optimising a factory or rescuing a stranded Duna lander.

interests.name: Programming
keywords: I build watch faces for my FitBit Versa, design programming languages, write flight computers for Kerbal Space Program, and experiment with esoteric hardware designs like balanced ternary logic. The thing I'm most proud of is the shotcut automation pipeline that backs my YouTube workflow.

interests.name: Language Learning
keywords: I'm learning Japanese. Currently at a beginner level and making slow but steady progress — it's a beautiful language and a welcome challenge after a day of writing Python.

interests.name: Amateur Astronomy
keywords: Exoplanet transit timing and observation through the British Astronomical Association. There's something magical about catching a tiny dip in a star's brightness from my back garden, knowing I'm watching another world pass by.

interests.name: Tea
keywords: Proper loose-leaf, brewed in a pot, left for exactly the right amount of time. I'm a firm believer that a good cup of tea solves most problems — or at least makes them easier to think about.

interests.name: Writing
keywords: Academic papers, documentation, and the occasional blog post. I find that explaining something clearly is the best way to make sure I understand it myself.

interests.name: Board Games
keywords: I love a good engine-building or co-op game, and I'm currently working on designing one of my own. It turns out that balancing a game is a lot like balancing a build pipeline — just with more dice.

publications.name: Exoclock Data Release IV
publications.description: Peer-reviewed paper in the ExoClock collaboration's Data Release IV, presenting a homogeneous catalogue of 620 updated exoplanet ephemerides. Contributed observational data and transit timing analysis from the TransitProject pipeline. Published on arXiv.

publications.name: Exoplanet TTV Masters Thesis
publications.description: Masters: Determining The Parameters of Exoplanetary Candidates From Transit Timing Variations.'

publications.name: IMBH Glitch Detection Bachelors Thesis
publications.description: Bachelors: Distinguishing Intermediate Mass Black Hole Mergers From Short Duration Glitches.'

publications.name: Extrasolar Habitation EPQ
publications.description: Extended Project: Is There A Possibility of Extrasolar Habitation?'

projects.name: Terraform MAAS bootstrap
projects.description: Three-stage Terraform module to bootstrap Juju on LXD, deploy charmed MAAS, and configure the deployed MAAS instance — enabling full infrastructure-as-code deployment from bare metal to a running MAAS cluster.

projects.name: MAAS Terraform provider
projects.description: Terraform provider published on the official Terraform Registry enabling CRUD management of MAAS resources including networking, users, machines, VM hosts, and DNS.

projects.name: MAAS Charms
projects.description: Charmed operators that automate the operational procedures of running MAAS — handling deployment, scaling, upgrades, and Day-2 operations of MAAS region and rack controllers.

projects.name: MAAS Anvil
projects.description: Snap-based charmed operator for deploying and managing MAAS as a Juju topology, orchestrated through Terraform-driven configuration workflows.

projects.name: Go MAAS Client
projects.description: Go client library providing a typed wrapper around the full MAAS REST API, used internally by the Terraform provider and other Go-based MAAS tooling.

projects.name: MAAS Internal CI
projects.description: Owner and primary maintainer of the MAAS team's Jenkins CI infrastructure, managing automated build, test, and release pipelines across multiple MAAS product repositories.

projects.name: Packer-MAAS
projects.description: Packer templates, scripts, and configuration for building custom deployable OS images for MAAS, supporting multiple operating systems and cloud-init integration.

projects.name: Metal As A Service
projects.description: MAAS transforms physical servers into cloud-like resources, automating provisioning, monitoring, and management of bare metal. Enables remote OS deployment, DHCP, DNS, IPAM, and full lifecycle management of data center hardware.

projects.name: YT-Automation
projects.description: Python-based automation suite for YouTube channel management. Handles automatic playlist population from video titles, two-way Google Calendar synchronisation for release scheduling, and automated background music selection for video editing in Shotcut. Uses the YouTube Data API via python-youtube and Google Calendar API with OAuth authentication.

projects.name: Ambistream
projects.description: Locally-hosted web application that streams YouTube audio to Bluetooth-connected devices, curated by weather and time of day. Successor to AutoBreezeBeats (2024), adding an orchestrator for automatic queuing from a database, a song metadata UI, and multi-process architecture managed through nox. Uses OpenWeatherMap API for context-aware music selection and ffmpeg for audio processing.

projects.name: VisualNovel Template
projects.description: A lightweight web-based Visual Novel engine template, supporting branching storylines, dynamic effects, and variations to ensure every playthrough feels unique. Stories are written in a simple markup format and compiled using a Python-based generator with built-in validation.

projects.name: Documentation Linting Tools
projects.description: CLI tooling providing automated documentation quality checks for the MAAS team. Wraps aspell, proselint, linkchecker, and style-checking utilities in a unified interface, enabling CI pipeline integration for doc PRs.

projects.name: Terraform Charmed MAAS Training
projects.description: Hands-on training material created and delivered for the Canonical Berlin Commercial Sprint 2025. Inducts field engineers in deploying and managing MAAS through the new Anvil approach using Terraform, Juju, and charmed operators, with step-by-step exercises for each deployment stage.

projects.name: Skiylia Programming Language
projects.description: A dynamically-typed, object-oriented, bytecode-compiled programming language implemented in Python. Features a custom lexer, parser, bytecode compiler, and virtual machine with garbage collection. Includes comprehensive documentation hosted on ReadTheDocs, a standard library, and release management with tagged versions. Open-source with contributions from the community.

projects.name: KOS Flight Computer
projects.description: Flight computer system written in KerboScript for the kOS (Kerbal Operating System) mod in Kerbal Space Program, designed for Realism Overhaul saves. Provides a multi-size BootUI system for connection-loss displays, real-time telemetry (MET, power, orbit status, disk capacity), and a modular menu interface for controlling spacecraft functions. Written entirely in KerboScript with ~400B of code.

projects.name: Season Clock
projects.description: Fitbit clock face displaying seasonal weather information and time. Designed for SDK 4.x devices with a focus on minimalist weather-aware aesthetics.

projects.name: Balanced Ternary Computer
projects.description: Exploratory project designing a balanced ternary logic computer from first principles. Covers radix economy comparisons between binary and ternary, balanced ternary negation and addition circuits, and practical considerations for hardware implementation. Documented in a technical blog post series.

projects.name: `Variations on an Exoplanet Theme' Webinar
projects.description: Computational Modelling of Transit Timing Variations

projects.name: Improved TTV Pipeline
projects.description: Enhanced pipeline for fitting transit timing variation (TTV) signals to exoplanetary data, building on the methodology developed in the Masters thesis. Improves signal detection and parameter estimation for non-transiting planet perturbations through refined analytical models.

projects.name: MAAS Ansible Playbooks
projects.description: An Ansible playbook for full installation and configuration of MAAS, providing automated bootstrapping of the MAAS snap, initial admin user, and networking setup.

projects.name: Data Science Project
projects.description: End-to-end data science pipeline predicting football match outcomes, covering: EDA and data cleaning with Pandas; feature engineering for team performance metrics; remote database upload via AWS RDS, PostgreSQL, and SQLAlchemy; model training and selection using sklearn (Logistic Regression, MLP, Naive Bayes, Decision Tree) with feature selection via SelectKBest; and inference on new season data.

projects.name: Data Collection Pipeline
projects.description: Full-stack cloud-based web scraper targeting the NASA Exoplanet Catalogue. Built with Selenium 4 for dynamic page interaction, UUID v4/JSON for data storage, and Boto3 for AWS S3 upload. Containerised with Docker and deployed on EC2 with Selenium Grid for distributed scraping. Monitored via Prometheus and Grafana dashboards with automated CI/CD and cron-based daily restarts. Scrapes all 4940+ known exoplanets.

projects.name: Fitbit Pokétch
projects.description: Fitbit clock face replicating the Pok\'etch from Pok\'emon Diamond/Pearl/Platinum, featuring 7 switchable applications: digital/analogue clocks, pedometer, stats view, manual counter, type-effectiveness calculator, calendar, and timer. Supports extensive customisation including button style, face/edge/screen colours, and mascot choice. Published on the Fitbit Gallery for both SDK 4.x (Versa 1/2/Lite) and SDK 6.0 (Versa 3/Sense).

projects.name: Computer Vision Rock-Paper-Scissors
projects.description: Interactive Rock-Paper-Scissors game using computer vision. A Keras/TensorFlow model trained via Google Teachable-Machine recognises four gestures (rock, paper, scissors, null) in real-time through a webcam feed processed with OpenCV. Includes both a terminal-based fallback and the full CV-enabled game loop.
